text: Welcome to Fragmented, an Android developer podcast where we talk about building good software and becoming better developers.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: I'm your host Kaushik.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: That's been the intro for over 200 episodes.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: Today, that's about to change.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: This is a pivot episode.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: When I started Fragmented years ago, I was an Android developer who couldn't find enough Android developer podcasts to listen to.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: I joke about this, but in my commute to work, I used to listen to more iOS podcasts because I was so desperate for good developer content.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: There was a clear gap.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: And we started Fragmented to fill that gap.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: I'm feeling that same feeling right now, if I'm being honest.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: That same gap, but maybe much, much bigger.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: Everyone is talking about AI.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: But almost no one is talking about AI the way working software engineers need to hear it.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: Here's what I mean.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: There's this term floating around that's set our entire world on fire.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: Vibe coding.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: And look, vibe coding is real and it's powerful.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: If you're not a developer or you're prototyping ideas or you just want to go from zero to one, AI can get you there fast.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: That is a real and fantastic development of these times.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: But how do you go from one to a hundred?
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: How do you leverage AI to even go from a one to a thousand?
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: That's the question not enough people are answering.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: We need to know how to use AI, not just for the prototypes, but
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: but for maintaining large codebases, legacy codebases that just run the world today.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: How do you build software for that?
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: How do you also keep it maintainable, reviewable, secure?
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: It's important that this software doesn't collapse when someone else has to touch it six months later.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: We need to build software in a way that a team of engineers can work on it simultaneously and not scratch their heads because someone vibe-coded an entire part of the codebase without really knowing the guts of the system well.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: It comes back to that point.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: Vibe-coding is great for demos.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: But let's be honest, software engineering is the job.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: And I don't see anyone bridging that chasm.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: That's what I want Fragmented the show to be about.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: Some of my proudest moments from Fragmented, and I mean this, is when folks walk up to me at a conference or send me a message and say, listening to your podcast helped me get my first job.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: To me, that's not a small thing.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: It helped me understand my purpose and drive for doing this show.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: So when I look at how dramatically the industry is shifting right now, it almost feels irresponsible to stay quiet about the developments in AI.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: To keep doing what we were doing and then just pretend that the ground beneath us isn't completely shifting is like Wile E. Coyote just running off a cliff.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: I see this shift in my day job too.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: We're asking questions like, which tasks should AI touch?
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: How do we help engineers be more productive by not just doing the things that they always used to do before?
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: What do productivity gains in this new world look like?
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: But importantly, how do we not get trapped in an avalanche of AI slop and unreviewable diffs?
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: In this probabilistic world, how do we evaluate outputs and keep the critical pieces of software deterministic?
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: These are real questions, and I don't think enough people are talking about them publicly, at least not in a way that helps working engineers navigate this new world order.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: And it's easy to be skeptical today about a podcast pivoting to talking about AI.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: So let me also tell you what this show is not going to be about.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: It's not an AI news show.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: There's plenty of newsletters and shows for that.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: I don't want to just recap or regurgitate that latest announcement made by the big AI labs without also critically thinking through how each of those changes and developments affect you as a developer.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: There's plenty of hype already.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: We are not going to tell you that AI will change your life if you just blindly follow these instructions and paste this prompt into your terminal.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: Don't get me wrong.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: We will definitely share tips and tricks and talk tactics.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: But we'll also mention the failure modes and what doesn't work.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: That's equally important.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: That's the stuff that you actually have to contend with when you go back to work and try to ship software.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: Now, for those of you who've been here since the Android days, I'm still a mobile developer.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: That hasn't changed.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: I'm still mostly paid to work on an Android operating system.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: So here's a small bonus.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: Every now and then, I'll bring in how you're using AI in mobile specifically.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: I'm hoping long-time listeners will especially appreciate and enjoy those aspects on not just how AI is changing software as a whole, but also the flavor around mobile.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: All right, one more thing.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: Maybe the most exciting thing for both you, the listener, and me.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: I'm not doing this alone.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: I went looking for someone who understands that gap that I told you about.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: The gap between vibe coding and real engineering.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: Someone sharp.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: Someone who's been saying the right things about AI and isn't interested in hype for just hype's sake.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: Someone who's really staying ahead of the curve in terms of these developments.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: Honestly, I'm just as excited to learn from him as I am to host alongside him.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: I'd like to announce that Yuri Souza will be joining us as the new co-host of Fragmented.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: When I first met Yuri and asked him what he thought about AI, he said something that really made me crack up.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: I figured it would be great to have him start on Fragmented with a bang and just tell you his thoughts directly.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: So here's Yuri's thoughts on AI.
speaker: SPEAKER_01

text: I got a weird analogy for this, but I think it still works.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: So bear with me for this one.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: The fact is, a lot of people talking about AI sound a lot like high schoolers talking about sex.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: You know, remember in high school, there's always these popular kids loudly claiming they're doing it all the time.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: And suddenly everyone starts saying the same thing just because that's kind of what you're supposed to say.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: But then years later at a reunion, you find that half of them were completely full of BS.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: That's really what a lot of AI-related content sounds like nowadays.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: There's people claiming they have five agents building apps while they sleep, these perpetual orchestration flows, these iShip assassin weekend stories.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: But then as soon as you start looking for more info, it all sounds very narrow or not even remotely grounded to what you'd expect to do in the context of your day job, right?
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: So our goal here is to bridge that gap and to get into the details of how you can learn to use the tools.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: Because AI is not only meant to do the work for you.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: AI can also augment your capacity to ship high-quality code.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: And I think that's the real leverage.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: And we have a lot of ground to cover.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: And I'm super humbled and excited to join Kaushik on this and to share it with you.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: Told you he was sharp.
